title: crotalaria pumila
intro: Crotalaria pumila, also known as dwarf crotalaria or short-pea bush, is a small, shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Crotalaria pumila include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antimalarial and anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and made into capsules or tinctures for more convenient consumption.
